steps.title: Identify the antecedent the pronoun must match
steps.content: The sentence reads "______ will begin to combust." The preceding sentence names "the substance" (chicken fat) — a singular, non-human noun. The pronoun must refer back to "the substance" and therefore must be singular and non-human.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — those: plural demonstrative pronoun; "the substance" is singular. ✗
  • Choice B — they: plural personal pronoun; same number mismatch. ✗
  • Choice C — that: singular demonstrative, but "that will begin to combust" sounds as if it introduces a new subject rather than referring back to one already named; non-standard for simple back-reference. ✗
  • Choice D — it: singular personal pronoun for non-human nouns; correctly refers back to the singular "the substance." ✓
That is the correct answer.
